.ts.purpose: A typed JavaScript file — the main language for modern web development

.tsx.purpose: Defines a visual component — what users see and interact with

.js.purpose: A script that runs in the browser or on a server

.jsx.purpose: A visual component written in JavaScript

.py.purpose: A Python script — versatile, used for everything from AI to web servers

.rs.purpose: A high-performance, safe systems language — used for fast, reliable software

.go.purpose: A fast, simple language by Google — popular for servers and cloud tools

.java.purpose: An enterprise language — runs on nearly every platform

.swift.purpose: Apple's language for building iOS and macOS apps

.kt.purpose: A modern language for Android apps and server-side development

.rb.purpose: A developer-friendly language — popular for web apps with Rails

.php.purpose: A web server language — powers much of the internet including WordPress

.c.purpose: A foundational language — the bedrock many other languages are built on

.cpp.purpose: A performance-critical language — used in games, browsers, and operating systems

.css.purpose: Controls visual styling — colors, layout, fonts, and spacing

.scss.purpose: Enhanced CSS with variables and nesting — makes styling more organized

.html.purpose: The skeleton of a web page — defines the structure and content

.json.purpose: Structured data — settings, configuration, or data exchange format

.yaml.purpose: Human-readable configuration — used for deployment and settings files

.yml.purpose: Human-readable configuration — used for deployment and settings files

.toml.purpose: Configuration format — clean and easy to read

.md.purpose: Documentation — formatted text for READMEs and notes

.sh.purpose: Automation — a batch of terminal commands run together

.sql.purpose: Database queries — reads and writes data from structured databases

.graphql.purpose: API query definitions — describes what data to fetch

.env.purpose: Secret settings — API keys and passwords (should never be shared)

.dockerfile.purpose: Container instructions — recipe for building a Docker image

.svg.purpose: Scalable graphic — an image that stays sharp at any size

.ipynb.purpose: Interactive computing — combines code, text, and visualizations
